summary: PR 2539 decompiled and improved code in vi0501 and sysdolphin psdisp. It replaced the stub for un_8031D9F8, matched particleSort according to the automated report, improved psDispParticles, added psdisp point/trail inline helpers, and deleted a temporary target_funcs.txt checklist.

key_files.role: Recovered un_8031D9F8 from a stub, added required subsystem includes, static HSD_GObj storage, player/stage setup calls, a Kirby spawn loop, JObj animation/translation access, and audio setup.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/vi/vi0501.c added 82 deleted 1 in 4 hunks
raw_diff.diff: vi0501.c replaces /// #un_8031D9F8 with a full function body
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report says main/melee/vi/vi0501 un_8031D9F8 improved +640 bytes, 0.00% to 98.83%; .sdata +16 reached 100%

key_files.role: Reworked particle sorting and particle display code, including natural loops over sort buckets, inline color helper wrappers, new psDispSubPoint and psDispSubPointTrail helpers, and control-flow/local lifetime changes in psDispParticles.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/sysdolphin/baselib/psdisp.c added 641 deleted 267 in 17 hunks
raw_diff.diff: particleSort unrolled bucket handling replaced by for loops over buckets[0..7] and buckets[8..15]
raw_diff.diff: new static inline getColorMatAmb_inner/getColorMatAmb_noinline, psDispSubPoint, and psDispSubPointTrail
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report says particleSort reached 100.00% and psDispParticles improved 39.03% to 58.06%

key_files.role: Deleted a temporary target checklist for mncharsel functions.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: target_funcs.txt added 0 deleted 13 in 1 hunk
raw_diff.diff: target_funcs.txt deleted file mode 100644 with mnCharSel checklist entries

what_changed: Implemented vi0501 function un_8031D9F8 with setup for camera, collision, ground/stage, items, fighter demo allocation, player initialization, Kirby demo spawn loop, JObj animation sampling, scaled position writes, and audio calls.
Added vi0501 includes for camera, fighter demo, ground/stage, item, collision, player, baselib gobj, and baselib jobj to support the recovered body.
Added static HSD_GObj* un_804A2E98[4] in vi0501.c for entities used by the un_8031D9F8 loop.
In particleSort, replaced a long hand-unrolled sequence over 16 particle sort buckets with two indexed for loops covering buckets 0-7 and 8-15.

Added psdisp inline wrapper helpers around getColorMatAmb and new inline helpers psDispSubPoint and psDispSubPointTrail that batch point/trail particle drawing through stack buffers and GXWGFifo writes.

Adjusted psDispParticles local variable/lifetime and control flow, including moving cache initialization earlier and changing channel-state branching from a switch-like form to nested conditionals in the shown diff excerpt.
Deleted target_funcs.txt, which contained a completed mncharsel target checklist unrelated to the two source files in the PR title.

smart_moves.text: The particleSort cleanup uses bounded indexed loops for repeated bucket-linking logic instead of preserving generated-looking repeated blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: particleSort buckets[0..7] and buckets[8..15] handled by for loops
decomp_standards:natural-loops

smart_moves.text: The vi0501 recovery keeps uncertain global/function names in address-style form while using clearer local role names such as jobj, pos, counts, scale, and v.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: un_8031D9F8 local declarations and calls to un_804A2E98/un_804D6F80
decomp_standards:conservative-naming

smart_moves.text: The PR scopes target-list cleanup separately by deleting target_funcs.txt rather than mixing stale checklist entries with active matching targets.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: target_funcs.txt deleted file mode 100644

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a particle or bucket routine has repeated blocks that differ only by bucket index, try small fixed-bound for loops before accepting unrolled decompiler output.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: particleSort replaces repeated bucket checks with for (i = 0; i < 8; i++) and for (i = 8; i < 16; i++)
text_corpus.jsonl: particleSort reported as 100.00% after PR

decomp_lessons.lesson: For partially matched display routines, helper extraction into static inline functions can isolate repeated point/trail emission patterns while preserving callsite inlining opportunities.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: adds static inline psDispSubPoint and psDispSubPointTrail
text_corpus.jsonl: psDispParticles improved from 39.03% to 58.06%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Large recovered scene/demo setup functions may need broad subsystem includes, but unknown globals and address-style function names should remain conservative until semantics are proven.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: un_8031D9F8 adds includes and retains un_804D6F74, un_804D6F78, un_804A2E98 naming
decomp_standards:truthful-headers-and-includes
decomp_standards:conservative-naming

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names were retained for uncertain recovered symbols, such as un_8031D9F8 and un_804A2E98, while local variables used role names once their role was evident.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: static HSD_GObj* un_804A2E98[4]
raw_diff.diff: locals jobj, pos, counts, scale, v in un_8031D9F8

naming_conventions.convention: New psdisp helpers use descriptive particle-display names with local subsystem prefixing: psDispSubPoint and psDispSubPointTrail.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: static inline HSD_Particle* psDispSubPoint
raw_diff.diff: static inline HSD_Particle* psDispSubPointTrail

naming_conventions.convention: The PR does not provide evidence for renaming unresolved globals or functions beyond the names shown in the diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: retained un_* identifiers in vi0501.c
decomp_standards:conservative-naming

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used natural fixed-count loops to replace repeated bucket-chain blocks in particleSort.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: particleSort for loops over buckets

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK(4) and explicit unused pad locals in un_8031D9F8, which is a stack-shaping tactic requiring verifier evidence before treating as ordinary style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: un_8031D9F8 declares pad0-pad4 and calls PAD_STACK(4)
decomp_standards:matching-tactics-need-evidence

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjusted local lifetime/register shape in psdisp by introducing new_var for &hsd_804D0908[arg0] and moving cache initialization earlier.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: temp_r29 = (new_var = &hsd_804D0908[arg0])
raw_diff.diff: cache = (f32*) &HSD_PSDisp_804D0FC0 moved before loop in psDispParticles

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced static inline wrapper layering around getColorMatAmb, including getColorMatAmb_inner and getColorMatAmb_noinline, likely to influence inline/call shape; evidence shows the tactic but not the author rationale.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: adds getColorMatAmb_inner and getColorMatAmb_noinline

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changed psDispParticles channel-state handling from the shown switch-style code to nested conditionals, suggesting control-flow shape was adjusted for matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: switch(chan_state) excerpt replaced by nested if checks

accepted_candidate_records.title: Fixed-bound loops can replace generated-looking bucket unrolls
accepted_candidate_records.text: In psdisp particleSort, repeated bucket-stitching blocks over 16 buckets were replaced with two for loops over ranges 0-7 and 8-15, and the automated report marked particleSort as 100.00%. This is a concrete example supporting the existing natural-loops standard.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: particleSort loop replacement
text_corpus.jsonl: particleSort +75 bytes, 92.88% to 100.00%

accepted_candidate_records.title: Conservative names retained while local roles are clarified
accepted_candidate_records.text: The vi0501 recovery kept unresolved address-style symbols while using local role names like jobj, pos, counts, and scale inside the recovered function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: un_8031D9F8 declarations and retained un_* symbols

Do not promote the PAD_STACK, new_var, cache lifetime move, or inline wrapper layering as general style; they are matching tactics with limited evidence in this slice.
Do not rename un_8031D9F8, un_804A2E98, or other address-style symbols based only on this PR.
